business.headline: Decoding the AI threat to banks that Nirmala Sitharaman warned aboutFinance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman met bank chiefs to discuss rising AI-linked risks in the financial system. To understand how serious the threat could become, we dug deeper into the risks banks face from AI and spoke to experts across the cybersecurity and technology sectors.

RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank licence: What it means for Paytm usersThe RBI has cancelled Paytm Payments Bank's licence after years of restrictions and scrutiny. The big question now is how this move could affect everyday Paytm users.

The many ways motor insurance is mis-sold in IndiaMotor insurance mis-selling in India is not an exception, it is part of how the system works. What customers don't know at the time of purchase often costs them the most at the time of claim.

Reliance Q4 profit slips to Rs 16,971 crore; Rs 6 dividend announcedReliance Industries posted consolidated net profit attributable to owners of Rs 16,971 crore for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, down from Rs 19,407 crore a year earlier.

RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank licence; to seek winding up in courtThe action marks a dramatic escalation in regulatory steps against Paytm Payments Bank, which had already faced severe business restrictions over the past two years.

Is not using your credit card doing more harm than good?For many people, the safest way to avoid debt is simple, i.e., stop using credit cards altogether. No swipes, no bills, no worries. But could this seemingly smart move actually be harming your credit score?

How this 25-year old built a Rs 20 lakh portfolio in almost 3 yearsBuilding a Rs 20 lakh portfolio usually feels like a long-term goal. But a 25-year-old techie managed to do it in just about three years. The surprising part? His salary wasn't very high, so what did he do differently?
Sensex ends 982 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IT stocks tankSensex ends 982 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IT stocks tank

Bernstein flags job risk, subsidy burden in open letter to PM ModiThe letter, which revisits themes first raised in 2019, comes at a time when India has moved up global GDP rankings but continues to face challenges in employment, innovation and infrastructure.

Should you pause your SIP when markets fall? Here's the truth most investors missThe moment markets dip, doubt creeps in. That steady SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) you trusted now feels risky. Should you stop and play safe for a while? The answer isn't as obvious as it seems.

Infosys grants Rs 51.75 crore ESOPs to CEO, employees still await pay hikeThe ESOP approval comes after a strong quarterly performance by the company. Infosys reported a 20.9% rise in net profit to Rs 8,501 crore in the March quarter, while revenue grew 13.4%.

Explained: How Rs 5 lakh PF withdrawal after 3 years could cost you Rs 1.5 lakhWithdrawing your PF after switching jobs may seem like an easy financial move. But what many don't realise is that an early withdrawal can come with a surprisingly high tax cost, cutting a big chunk from your savings.
Why HCLTech shares are under pressure againThe fresh decline comes just days after the company's latest quarterly results disappointed the Street and triggered a sharp sell-off in the stock.

Received money, mutual funds or shares as gifts? Don't miss these ITR rulesGifts in the form of money or investments are quite common, but they come with tax rules that are often overlooked. Knowing what to declare in your ITR can save you from unnecessary notices later.

8th Pay Commission: Key meetings to begin in Delhi from April 28, more rounds plannedIn a notice dated April 24, 2026, the Commission said it has received a large number of requests from employee unions and associations for meetings scheduled between April 28 and April 30 in Delhi.

Infosys share price crashes after Q4 results: Why stock fell 6% todayThe IT giant's share price declined 6% intraday, making it one of the biggest losers in the technology pack, as investors reacted more to future guidance than to the quarterly profit itself.

AI security risk for banks? Why has Nirmala Sitharaman raised concernsAt the centre of the issue is Anthropic's new AI model, known as Claude Mythos Preview, capable of identifying and exploiting vulnerabilities across major operating systems and web browsers when prompted.

Infosys shares down 5%, hit 52-week low: Why IT stock tumbled after Q4 resultsAt around 11:01 am, Infosys shares were trading at Rs 1,178.20 on the NSE, down 5.03% or Rs 62.40 for the day. The fall made it one of the worst performers on benchmark indices as the IT pack remained under pressure.

Will petrol, diesel prices rise after elections? Here's what we knowThe government has dismissed rumours of a petrol and diesel price hike, but elevated crude prices and pressure on oil retailers have kept the debate firmly alive.

HCLTech, TCS, Infosys: Why are IT stocks falling again?HCLTech led losses as frontline IT stocks fell up nearly 5% amid renewed worries over global demand, weak guidance and continued foreign investor selling pressure.

entertainment.headline: Bunny meets Avi: Ranbir–Aditya reunion sparks Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani nostalgia Actors Ranbir Kapoor and Aditya Roy Kapur were seen together in Mumbai, much to the delight of Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani fans. The sighting revived memories of their characters, Bunny and Avi, as the film continues to enjoy strong fan love.

Wheel of Fortune finale: Akshay Kumar gets teary-eyed on stage after tribute A tribute video during the Wheel of Fortune India finale shoot left Akshay Kumar visibly emotional. The actor reflected on the show's journey and thanked viewers for their support.

Viral video: Rihanna learns Bharatanatyam mudras during Mumbai visit International pop star Rihanna tried mastering Bharatanatyam during her Mumbai visit. A video of her learning mudras and expressions of the classical dance form is now being widely shared online.

Mohanlal's viral moment with The Chainsmokers: Cinema meets the sound of music Actor Mohanlal shared a picture with The Chainsmokers, after meeting the American DJ duo in Dallas. The unexpected crossover quickly caught the attention of fans online. The duo called the actor a friend.

KJo's double bonanza for fans: Kabhi Alvida Na Kehna series, Koffee with Karan 9 Filmmaker and show host Karan Johar will adapt his 2006 film, Kabhi Alvida Naa Kehna, into a long-form series for a streaming platform. He also confirmed Koffee with Karan would return this year with Season 9.

The Kerala Story 2 on OTT: When and where to watch the controversial film The Kerala Story 2, starring Ulka Gupta, Aditi Bhatia and Aishwarya Ojha, is set to premiere online. The controversial sequel explores stories of conversion beyond Kerala, and saw a modest box office run.

Found the greatest man: Casting director Shanoo Sharma announces wedding. Pics Casting director Shanoo Sharma announced her wedding on social media and shared photographs from the ceremony. The post drew congratulations from film personalities, including Ranveer Singh, Parineeti Chopra and others.

Orry jokes about Rihanna not returning earrings after Mumbai reunion Influencer Orry reunited with pop star Rihanna in Mumbai and joked that she still has the crystal earrings he gave her in Jamnagar. The playful caption revived a viral pre-wedding moment from Jamanagar.

Rihanna-Parul Gulati viral photo sparks buzz, actor clears the air Actor and entrepreneur Parul Gulati posted pictures on Instagram that seemed to show her with pop star Rihanna. The post drew quick reactions online and renewed attention on digital authenticity as Rihanna's Mumbai visit stayed in focus.

Michael Jackson's 'second family' files lawsuit alleging sexual abuse of four kids The Cascios, a family once described as Michael Jackson's second family, have filed a lawsuit alleging years of sexual abuse. The case revives scrutiny of the singer as his biopic Michael reaches cinemas.

Rashmika, Vijay cheer for fan's viral Vachindamma reel: Timing is epic Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda reacted to an influencer's recreation of the song, Vachindamma from Geetha Govindam. The duo praised the talent and timing of the young fan.

Unchosen review: Atmospheric cult drama led by strong performances and slow pacingRelease Date:Tue Apr 21, 2026 Director Jim Loach and creator Julie Gearey deliver Unchosen, a haunting look at faith and control. Despite a slow pace, Molly Windsor's quiet rebellion makes this psychological thriller series deeply immersive.

Rihanna asks how to say thank you in Hindi; wins hearts with shukriya in Mumbai Rihanna launched her cosmetic brand in Mumbai and later thanked fans in Hindi. Her fun interaction, saying Shukriya quickly went viral online.

Rihanna's viral Mumbai moment: Singer invites pap for a photo-op in fun video Barbadian singer and businesswoman Rihanna invited a photographer to join her on the red carpet at a Mumbai event. The moment went viral, with fans praising her warm exchange with the paparazzi.

Raaka, Pushpa, AA23: Why Allu Arjun is Tollywood's superstar shape-shifter After the Pushpa films, Allu Arjun continues ditching the playbook with Atlee's Raaka. He steps into a bold, unrecognisable avatar again. In this week's Cinematic Saturday, we decode how it's a sharp pivot – less comfort, more risk – and a clear sign he's rewriting his superstardom.

Ahaan Panday on why he took Aneet Padda to places of worship before Saiyaara release Ahaan Panday said he took Aneet Padda to places of worship before Saiyaara was released in theatres. He said that the visits were not meant to pray for a hit.

Rob, Michele Reiner's son Jake reflects on parents' deaths: A living nightmare Actor Jake Reiner opened up about the shock and grief of losing his parents, filmmaker Rob Reiner and his wife Michele Singer. He reflected on coping with the tragedy and asked for compassion as his brother Nick faces murder charges.

Sanjay Dutt announces Khalnayak Returns, Maanayata can't control her tears Maanayata Dutt fought tears as her husband Sanjay Dutt unveiled Khalnayak Returns in Mumbai on Friday. She recalled the highs and lows the actor had to go through to realise the project.

Ssunita Ahuja, Krushna-Kashmera reunion on Laughter Chefs 3 ends 14-year feud. Watch Govinda's wife, Ssunita Ahuja, will be seen with Krushna Abhishek and Kashmera Shah on Laughter Chefs 3 after years of feud. The emotional moment saw apologies, forgiveness, and differing takes on what the meeting meant.

general news.headline: Too much time wasted: Trump scraps envoys' Pak trip, says no more 18-hour flightsUS President Donald Trump said that they hold all the cards in the war against Iran, hence his delegation is not going to make the 18-hour-long journey to Islamabad for the second round of discussions.

Iran Foreign Minister Araghchi expected to revisit Pakistan after Oman talksIran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to return to Pakistan after talks in Oman before travelling to Russia, according to reports. The visit sequence keeps Pakistan at the centre of indirect diplomacy as negotiations remain stalled.

Angry Mamata Banerjee walks off stage over noise from BJP rally 100 metres awayFollowing her departure, angry TMC workers marched toward Adhikari's rally to protest, leading to a heated exchange of slogans between supporters of both sides. The situation quickly escalated, prompting security forces to intervene and restore order.

Ngidi stable after nasty head injury, set to be discharged from hospital soonDC vs PBKS: Lungi Ngidi is stable and set to be discharged after being taken to hospital with head and neck pain following a nasty fall during Delhi Capitals' IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings.

China's nail house resistance seen at Delhi-Dehradun expresswayIn China, authorities have built highways around "nail houses" after landowners refused to sell them. A similar case is now visible in India, where a house in Ghaziabad is blocking a key exit ramp on the Delhi–Dehradun Expressway.

At 42.8 degrees Celsius, Delhi records its hottest day of the yearA yellow alert had been issued for Saturday, and a similar warning remains in place for Sunday, advising caution due to isolated heatwave conditions. A heatwave is defined as a maximum temperature of 40°C or higher, with a deviation of 4.5 degrees Celsius to 6.4 degrees Celsius above normal.

Biggest rule change in badminton history: BWF approves new scoring systemThe Badminton World Federation has approved the biggest rule change in modern badminton, replacing the long-standing 3x21 scoring format with a new 3x15 system from January 2027. While BWF says the move will improve player welfare and make matches more exciting, former world No. 1 Saina Nehwal had warned against changes that could affect the spirit of the sport.

'Sheesh Mahal 2' pics taken from Pinterest? Atishi 'exposes' Parvesh VermaThe Sheesh Mahal controversy is back in Delhi. After BJP minister Parvesh Verma shared images from what he claimed was Arvind Kejriwal's new bungalow, the AAP alleged that the images were lifted from Pinterest.

Viral video: Rihanna learns Bharatanatyam mudras during Mumbai visitInternational pop star Rihanna tried mastering Bharatanatyam during her Mumbai visit. A video of her learning mudras and expressions of the classical dance form is now being widely shared online.

How cops created green corridor, rushed Ngidi to hospital in 11 mins after injuryLungisani Ngidi was taken to hospital after a heavy fall while attempting a catch during the Delhi-Punjab IPL match. Delhi Traffic Police created a green corridor, and early updates said he was stable.

Gen Z effect? Raghav Chadha loses 1 million followers after BJP switchThe shock exit of Raghav Chadha from the AAP to join the BJP doesn't seem to have impressed Gen Z. The suave Rajya Sabha MP, who enjoyed considerable popularity among the youth, lost one million followers in under 24 hours.

Rs 299 dress on Facebook turns into Rs 1 lakh scam for Mumbai nurseA Mumbai nurse alleged she was cheated of Rs 1 lakh while trying to buy a Rs 299 dress online. The case has led Deonar police to examine a Facebook ad, a WhatsApp number and a fake delivery agent.

Video: DRDO unveils advanced armoured platform for land and water combatDRDO has rolled out the Vikram VT21 Advanced Armoured Platform in tracked and wheeled variants at Ahilyanagar. The indigenous system adds mobility, protection and firepower to India's combat vehicle push.

Live: Benjamin Netanyahu orders forceful strikes on Hezbollah in LebanonUS-Iran War Live: US President Donald Trump has called off a planned visit by American officials to Pakistan, effectively halting a second round of indirect negotiations with Iran. Speaking to Fox News, Trump dismissed the long journey, arguing that an 18-hour trip was unnecessary given what he described as the United States' strong negotiating position. He stated that his team had been preparing to depart, but he intervened, saying the journey would be unproductive. According to Trump, Washington holds decisive leverage and sees no reason to engage in lengthy discussions abroad when Tehran could initiate contact instead. The cancelled trip was expected to include envoy Steve Witkoff and Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner, who were scheduled to meet Iranian representatives in Islamabad. The development came shortly after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi concluded meetings with Pakistani leaders, including Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif. Iran has maintained it will reject what it calls excessive US demands and remains wary following past negotiations and subsequent military tensions. Although a fragile ceasefire currently exists, regional instability persists, particularly around energy supplies and disruptions linked to the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

Iran's Araghchi calls Pak visit fruitful, questions US seriousness on diplomacyIran has shown no inclination to hold direct talks with the United States even as the conflict has entered its ninth week. While US President Donald Trump has extended the ceasefire, Iranian sources have reiterated that Tehran won't accept "maximalist demands" by the US to end the war.

70-year-old man arrested for alleged rape of mentally disabled niece in GujaratPolice in Lunawada have arrested a 70-year-old man accused of raping his 26-year-old relative with a mental disability. The case has raised concern locally as investigators pursue charges under sexual assault and disability laws.

Beyond the spotlight: PM Modi's recurring moments behind the cameraA closer look at Prime Minister Narendra Modi's lesser-seen moments behind the camera, capturing wildlife, cityscapes, and candid scenes during official visits.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Patient enroute to hospital dies after petrol pump denies fuel to ambulanceThe ambulance allegedly remained stationed at the pump for about 15 minutes. However, records showed that the petrol pump had sufficient stock on that day.

Wore torn pants earlier, built Rs 100-crore house as CM: Swati Maliwal on KejriwalA day after joining the BJP from AAP, Swati Maliwal attacked Arvind Kejriwal and praised Prime Minister Narendra Modi. She said her decision to switch sides was voluntary and urged those seeking constructive politics to join the saffron party.

Cash rain on UP highway? Rs 4 lakh found scattered in Shamli, passersby alert copsBundles of currency notes were found scattered along a highway stretch in Shamli, prompting police to recover Rs 4 lakh. CCTV checks and bank outreach are under way as no one has yet come forward to claim the cash.

Helps the rich, not the poor: Rahul Gandhi puts Mamata, Modi in same bracketRahul Gandhi, addressing a rally in Serampore, accused both Mamata Banerjee and PM Modi of helping the rich instead of the poor, while hankering for power.

Left out of disgust, not fear: Raghav Chadha's fresh attack on AAP after exitA day after merging with the BJP, Raghav Chadha said he left the Aam Aadmi Party out of disgust, not fear. He said seven Rajya Sabha MPs acted together because they believed the party had strayed from its principles.

Father-son duo thrash neighbour's chained dog with pipe, stick in GujaratA Gujarat man has accused a father and son of entering his courtyard and beating his tied pet dog. The complaint has triggered a police probe and fresh outrage over alleged animal cruelty.

sports.headline: Dhoni regains full fitness, but wants to avoid return to preserve CSK's team balance CSK vs GT: MS Dhoni is fully fit ahead of CSK's clash against Gujarat Titans, but the veteran is unlikely to return immediately as he does not want to disrupt Chennai's settled team combination.

Deepti Sharma redeems herself, saves India from T20I series whitewash vs South Africa SAW vs INDW, 4th T20I: Deepti Sharma was under scrutiny for her form, but the all-rounder responded in style as India beat South Africa by 14 runs on Saturday at the Wanderers in Johannesburg.

Trump envoy wants Italy to replace Iran in FIFA World Cup. Country says nope Donald Trump's envoy for global relations, Paolo Zampolli, suggested that Italy should replace Iran at this year's FIFA World Cup, but the idea was swiftly rejected by Italian officials, who insisted that qualification must be earned on the pitch and not through political influence.

Sooryavanshi storm in vain as Abhishek, Ishan leave RR parched in Jaipur RR vs SRH, IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's stunning second IPL ton lit up Jaipur, but SRH spoiled the 15-year-old's big night as Abhishek Sharma and Ishan Kishan powered a ruthless chase for Sunrisers Hyderabad.

Biggest rule change in badminton history: BWF approves new scoring system The Badminton World Federation has approved the biggest rule change in modern badminton, replacing the long-standing 3x21 scoring format with a new 3x15 system from January 2027. While BWF says the move will improve player welfare and make matches more exciting, former world No. 1 Saina Nehwal had warned against changes that could affect the spirit of the sport.

Axar lambasts DC after Punjab loss: Fans call Karun Nair the new Shashank Singh Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel lambasted his team for dropping catches against Punjab Kings. DC scored 264 runs, one of the highest totals in IPL history, but ended up surrendering the game to Shreyas Iyer's Punjab.

If they can, why can't we? Shreyas Iyer's unbreakable mindset for record chase Shreyas Iyer said Punjab Kings chased 265 against Delhi Capitals with a simple dressing-room message: score one more run. His remarks underlined the calm approach behind the highest successful chase in IPL history.

Heatwave strikes again: Vaibhav suffers injury scare after 36-ball 100 Young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was carried off the field while fielding in Rajasthan Royals' match against SunRisers Hyderabad. Fielding in the covers in the 4th over, Vaibhav was unable to continue and had to be carried off the field by the support staff.

An angry 15-year-old's revenge: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hits 36-ball hundred vs SRH Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stormed to his second hundred in the Indian Premier League on Saturday, April 25. Playing against SRH, Sooryavanshi smashed his way to a 36-ball hundred while playing in front of their home crowd in Jaipur.

South Africa vs India, 4th Women's T20I: Highlights from Johannesburg SAW vs INDW, 4th T20I Highlights: India won the fourth T20I by 14 runs at the Wanderers in Johannesburg after Deepti Sharma scored 36 runs and took her maiden five-wicket haul in the format.

Delhi's 264? No problem. Punjab remain unbeatable with IPL's biggest run chase IPL 2026, DC vs PBKS: KL Rahul's historic 152 went in vain as Punjab Kings chased down a record 265 in 18.5 overs. An ice-cool Shreyas Iyer's finishing act and Prabhsimran SIngh's opening blast ensured Punjab Kings went past the finish line and remained unbeaten this season.

Ngidi stable after nasty head injury, set to be discharged from hospital soonDC vs PBKS: Lungi Ngidi is stable and set to be discharged after being taken to hospital with head and neck pain following a nasty fall during Delhi Capitals' IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings.

RR vs SRH Highlights: Sunrisers beat Rajasthan by 5 wickets to continue impressive runRajasthan (RR) vs Hyderabad (SRH) Highlights, IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad won their fourth match on the trot after beating the Rajasthan Royals by five wickets at the Sawai Mansingh Stadium in Jaipur.

KL Rahul, DC unleash carnage on historic record-breaking day in New DelhiIPL 2026, DC vs PBKS: KL Rahul scored an unbeaten 152 as Delhi Capitals piled up a massive 264 for two against Punjab Kings after opting to bat first at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

Lungi Ngidi suffers nasty head injury, rushed to hospital on ambulanceIPL, DC vs PBKS: Lungi Ngidi suffered a nasty injury while fielding. He was stretchered off the field in an ambulance and later rushed to the hospital. The match between Punjab and Delhi was halted for 15 minutes as stunned players watched on in concern.

KL Rahul battered by Delhi heat, struggles to enjoy his finest IPL centuryDC vs PBKS: KL Rahul battled Delhi's brutal heat to produce a historic 150, but the physical toll was clear as the exhausted DC batter needed physio attention while keeping during Punjab Kings' chase.

152 off 67: KL Rahul sets new IPL record after hitting fastest century for DelhiIPL 2026, DC vs PBKS: KL Rahul, dropped on 12, went on to smash a 47-ball hundred for Delhi Capitals on Saturday. He was in complete control of proceedings, punishing the Punjab Kings spinners and pacers in the V at Arun Jaitley Stadium.

Just hide Shashank Singh: PBKS take player off after continued fielding howlersPunjab Kings took Shashank Singh off the field after the player continued to commit big errors in the field. After dropping 3 catches in the previous match, Shashank fumbled straightforward chances against Delhi as well.

IPL 2026: Mitchell Starc joins Delhi Capitals but misses Punjab Kings clashIPL 2026: Mitchell Starc is back in the Delhi Capitals camp, but his return to the XI will have to wait. With fitness and rhythm the priority, DC are choosing patience over urgency.

How can Padikkal get into Team India? Sehwag flags selection issue despite IPL formIPL 2026: Devdutt Padikkal is ticking every box this season with RCB, but his Team India selection is not just about form anymore. With India's top order packed, even standout performances may not guarantee a spot.

DC vs PBKS Highlights: Punjab Kings chase down record 265 runs in 18.5 oversDelhi (DC) vs Punjab (PBKS) Highlights, IPL 2026: Punjab Kings (265/4 in 18.5 ovs) chasing 265. Punjab Kings have chased down a record target of 265 runs in just 18.5 overs. Shreyas Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh guide Punjab to a ridiculous chase, the highest in T20 cricket history.

Shubman Gill, you have lost courage: Sehwag slams poor GT captaincy vs RCBIPL 2026: Virender Sehwag criticised Shubman Gill's captaincy after Gujarat Titans lost to Royal Challengers Bengaluru, questioning his bowling decisions in the death overs. He felt Gill missed a chance to extend the game by not using key bowlers at crucial moments.

This is the new Devdutt Padikkal: RCB star embraces his new fiery versionIPL 2026: Devdutt Padikkal has redefined his T20 game in IPL 2026, trading caution for controlled aggression. The new approach is not just eye-catching, it is delivering real impact for RCB.

Kohli, Siraj reunited: Fans loving heartwarming moment after RCB vs GTIPL 2026: A brief reunion between Virat Kohli and Mohammed Siraj became the standout moment after RCB's win over GT. The viral hug captured the bond between former teammates, striking a chord with fans in Bengaluru.

Kylian Mbappe faces World Cup scare after injury blow in Real Madrid's draw vs BetisKylian Mbappe went off with left hamstring discomfort during Real Madrid's 1-1 draw with Real Betis. The setback adds to Madrid's injury problems and raises fresh concern before France's World Cup opener.

Root better to watch than Virat Kohli? Ex-England captain sparks social media warIPL 2026: Michael Vaughan said Joe Root is better to watch than Virat Kohli after Sam Billings praised Kohli's latest RCB knock. The remark set off a social media debate as Kohli's franchise form and Root's red-ball class were weighed against each other.

With Pat Cummins boost, SRH set sights on extending impressive run vs RRIPL 2026, RR vs SRH: Sunrisers Hyderabad head to Jaipur seeking another win against Rajasthan Royals with Pat Cummins back in contention. The contest puts Rajasthan's top-order reliance under focus as both sides chase a stronger top-four position.

Captains from best to worst: An IPL 2026 mid-season reportShreyas Iyer has emerged as the standout captain so far, while Hardik Pandya sits at the bottom of the rankings. The mid-season assessment also tracks strong starts from Rajat Patidar and Ishan Kishan, and growing pressure on several others.

Sinner urges Alcaraz not to rush after French Open miss due to injuryJannik Sinner urged Carlos Alcaraz not to rush back after the Spaniard withdrew from Roland Garros with a wrist injury. The defending champion's absence opens up the Paris draw and shifts attention to whether he can return for Wimbledon.

The Man in the Mirror: How Virat Kohli's Ziddi spirit is outrunning time in IPLIPL 2026, RCB vs GT: Virat Kohli smashed a 44-ball 81 in Bengaluru's successful chase of 206 against Gujarat Titans on Friday. Gujarat Titans' assistant coach Vijay Dahiya spoke about the 'Ziddi spirit' that is helping Kohli stay ahead of the curve in this new-era of T20 cricket.

IPL Play of the Day: RCB's triple-threat choke that clamped GT in final stretchRCB vs GT, IPL Play of the Day: RCB flipped the script at the death, conceding just 17 runs in three overs as Hazlewood, Bhuvneshwar and Suyash choked the flow to keep the target firmly within reach.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
